exercises.title: Trace Optional.orElse
exercises.scenario: Present and empty Optional with orElse.
hints: orElse returns the value if present, otherwise the default
isPresent() returns true if has value

exercises.title: Optional.ofNullable wrapping
exercises.scenario: Safe wrapping of a potentially null value.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to safely wrap a value that might be null.

blanks.hint: Factory method that handles null safely

hints: Optional.of() throws NPE on null
Optional.ofNullable() converts null → empty Optional

exercises.title: ifPresent for side effects
exercises.scenario: Run action only if value is present.
exercises.instructions:
Use `ifPresent` to print a welcome message only if the user is found. Print 'User not found' if absent.

Expected output:
Welcome, Aisha!
User not found
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// ifPresent for conditional action
hints: found.ifPresent(name -> println...)
notFound: use ifPresentOrElse(present action, empty action) — Java 9+

exercises.title: Optional.map chain
exercises.scenario: Transform value through a chain of maps.
exercises.instructions:
Chain map operations on an Optional<String>: trim → uppercase → take first 5 chars. Handle empty Optional with orElse.

Test: '  hyderabad  ' and empty.

Expected output:
HYDER
NO DATA
hints: map(String::strip) → map(String::toUpperCase) → map(s -> s.substring(0,5))
empty Optional: map operations skipped, orElse returns 'NO DATA'

exercises.title: Optional.filter
exercises.scenario: Keep value only if it passes a condition.
exercises.instructions:
Use Optional.filter to validate an age value. Accept only ages 18-65. Return the age or -1 for invalid.

Test: 25, 15, 70, empty.

Expected output:
25
-1
-1
-1
hints: 25 passes; 15 < 18 fails; 70 > 65 fails; empty stays empty
All failures → orElse(-1)

exercises.title: orElse vs orElseGet
exercises.instructions:
Show the difference between orElse (always evaluates) and orElseGet (lazy). Count how many times a 'computation' runs.

Expected output:
orElse calls: present=1 absent=1
orElseGet calls: present=0 absent=1
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// orElse vs orElseGet evaluation count

test_cases.expected_stdout:
orElse calls: present=1 absent=1
orElseGet calls: present=0 absent=1

hints: orElse(compute()) — compute() is evaluated as an argument before orElse runs
orElseGet(() -> compute()) — lambda only runs if Optional is empty

exercises.title: Optional returned from method
exercises.scenario: Design a method that returns Optional instead of null.
exercises.instructions:
Refactor `findProductById(List<Product>, String id)` from returning null to returning Optional<Product>. Caller handles the Optional.

Expected output:
Found: Mouse ₹799.0
Not found: No product with ID 'X99'

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Found: Mouse ₹799.0
Not found: No product with ID 'X99'

hints: stream().filter(id match).findFirst() already returns Optional<Product>
ifPresentOrElse(present action, empty action)

exercises.title: Optional chain — null-safe property access
exercises.scenario: Replace nested null checks with Optional.map chain.
exercises.instructions:
A User has an Address (nullable) which has a PostalCode (nullable). Get the postal code or 'No postal code'.

Test: user with full address, user with null address, no user.

Expected output:
500032
No postal code
No postal code
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Optional chain: user → address → postalCode

test_cases.expected_stdout:
500032
No postal code
No postal code

hints: ofNullable(db.get(userId)) handles missing user
.map(u -> u.address) handles null address → empty Optional
.map(a -> a.postalCode) safe only if address is present

exercises.title: Optional.flatMap
exercises.scenario: Avoid Optional<Optional<T>> when chaining Optional-returning methods.
exercises.instructions:
A Company has an Optional<CEO> (ceo might be vacant). CEO has Optional<String> (bio might not be written). Chain to get 'company → CEO bio' or default.

Expected output:
TCS CEO bio: "Technology leader"
Startup CEO bio: Position vacant
Meta CEO bio: No bio written
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Optional.flatMap to avoid Optional<Optional<T>>

test_cases.expected_stdout:
TCS CEO bio: "Technology leader"
Startup CEO bio: Position vacant
Meta CEO bio: No bio written

hints: getCeo() returns Optional<CEO> — use flatMap with getBio() since getBio() also returns Optional
If map used: Optional<CEO>.map(getBio) → Optional<Optional<String>>
flatMap unwraps: Optional<CEO>.flatMap(getBio) → Optional<String>

exercises.title: Trace Optional chain
exercises.scenario: Follow value through filter and map operations.
hints: 'Hello' after strip = 5 chars, fails filter(> 10) → empty → 'filtered out'
'Hello World' after strip = 11 chars, passes filter → HELLO WORLD

exercises.title: orElseThrow with custom exception
exercises.scenario: Optional as a validated lookup with domain exception.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `findOrder(Map<String, Order>, String orderId)` that returns the Order or throws `OrderNotFoundException` (unchecked custom exception).

Expected output:
Found order: ₹3500.0
OrderNotFoundException: Order not found: ORD-999
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// orElseThrow with custom exception

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Found order: ₹3500.0
OrderNotFoundException: Order not found: ORD-999

hints: Optional.ofNullable(orders.get(orderId))
.orElseThrow(() -> new OrderNotFoundException('Order not found: ' + orderId))

exercises.title: Stream + Optional integration
exercises.scenario: Stream terminals return Optional — chain immediately.
exercises.instructions:
Find the most expensive in-stock product. If none, throw ProductNotFoundException. Chain stream + Optional directly.

Products: Mouse(₹799, in-stock), Monitor(₹18999, out-of-stock), USB Hub(₹499, in-stock)

Expected output:
Most expensive in-stock: Mouse ₹799.0
(no in-stock products case)
ProductNotFoundException: No in-stock products found

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Most expensive in-stock: Mouse ₹799.0
ProductNotFoundException: No in-stock products found

hints: filter(inStock) removes Monitor
max(comparingDouble(price)) returns Optional<Product>
.orElseThrow() throws if no in-stock products
Mouse(799) > USB Hub(499) → Mouse is most expensive in-stock

exercises.title: Null-safe deep chain refactor
exercises.scenario: Replace nested null checks with Optional chain.
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Refactor 10-line null chain to Optional chain
hints: 4 chained .map() calls, each extracting next level
Any null in the chain → Optional.empty() → skip rest → orElse('GLOBAL')
10 lines → 6 lines, no if-null needed

exercises.title: Optional in Spring-style repository pattern
exercises.scenario: Optional as repository return type — real Spring Data pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Implement a simple `UserRepository` where `findById` returns `Optional<User>`. Service layer uses it with orElseThrow. Controller layer calls service and returns formatted response.

Expected output:
GET /users/1: Welcome Aisha (Engineering)
GET /users/99: 404 User not found: id=99
GET /users/2: 403 Account suspended: Raj
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Spring-style Optional repository → service → controller

test_cases.expected_stdout:
GET /users/1: 200 Welcome Aisha (Engineering)
GET /users/99: 404 User not found: id=99
GET /users/2: 403 Account suspended: Raj

hints: findById returns Optional<User>
.orElseThrow() in service throws UserNotFoundException if absent
Controller catches specific exceptions and maps to HTTP codes

exercises.title: Complete Optional + Stream integration
exercises.scenario: Full Module 8 synthesis: streams + collectors + Optional.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `ProductService` that combines everything from Module 8:
1. `findCheapestByCategory(List<Product>, String cat)` — stream filter + min → Optional
2. `getCategoryReport(List<Product>)` — groupingBy + map over Optional values
3. `findAndTransform(List<Product>, String id)` — findFirst → Optional chain: map name → filter long → orElse
4. `allPricesForCategory(List<Product>, String cat)` — stream + collect joining

Expected output:
Cheapest Electronics: Mouse ₹799.0
Cheapest Books: Java Book ₹649.0
Category report: {Books=₹649.0-₹899.0, Electronics=₹799.0-₹1999.0}
Transformed ID P01: MOUSE
Transformed ID P99: SHORT_ID
Electronics prices: ₹799.0, ₹1999.0

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Cheapest Electronics: Mouse ₹799.0
Cheapest Books: Java Book ₹649.0
Category report: {Books=₹649.0-₹899.0, Electronics=₹799.0-₹1999.0}
Transformed ID P01: MOUSE
Transformed ID P99: SHORT_ID
Electronics prices: ₹799.0, ₹1999.0

hints: findCheapestByCategory: stream.filter.min returns Optional<Product>
getCategoryReport: groupingBy + collectingAndThen to compute range string
findAndTransform: findFirst → map name → map upper → filter ≥5 → orElse
'MOUSE' has 5 chars ≥ 5, passes filter. P99 not found → empty → 'SHORT_ID'
